chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Constitutional Petition No. D – 3940 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGES Priority: 1. For hearing of CMA No.16153/2025. 2. For hearing of main case. 11.08.2025 M/S. Fauzia Rasheed, Maimoona Nasreen and Bushra Zaheer Ahmed, Advocates for the Petitioner. Syed Bashir Hussain Shah, Asstt. Attorney General. -------------------------------------- Mr. Aamir Ali Shaikh Advocate files Vakalatnama for Respondent No.2. Against the Order in Original No.ONO-2020224 dated 31.07.2025, the Petitioner’s Appeal is pending before the Appellate Tribunal Customs bearing Customs appeal No.K- 1093/2025. The Petitioner prays that in the meantime the consignments may be released upon securing the differential amount of duty/taxes, fine and penalty. To that extent learned counsel for Respondent No.2 has no objection as there is no issue on the importability of the subject goods. Let the undisputed quantum of duties and taxes be deposited by the Petitioner with the Collectorate/Department and the disputed amount of the duties and taxes along with the adjudged fine and penalty be secured before the Nazir of this Court, by way of pay order(s) or bank guarantee, in respect of the consignment(s) subject matter of this petition. Once the amount is so secured, the Nazir shall issue the appropriate certificate(s). If the amount is secured by way of pay order(s), the Nazir shall invest the amount in profit bearing instrument, as per the Rules. The Nazir’s fee is settled at Rs.5,000/- per certificate. Upon deposit of the undisputed quantum of duties and taxes with the Collectorate/Department and presentation of the aforesaid

The Nazir’s fee is settled at Rs.5,000/- per certificate. Upon deposit of the undisputed quantum of duties and taxes with the Collectorate/Department and presentation of the aforesaid certificate(s) issued by the Nazir, the concerned Collectorate/Department shall release the consignment(s) forthwith. The fate of the amount secured supra shall be subject to the outcome of the appeal pending before the Appellate Tribunal. This Petition, along with pending application, is disposed of in said terms. JUDGE JUDGE Nadeem Qureshi P.A.
